steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between 'Japan' and 'respectively' and then continues into a new independent clause beginning with 'Utilizing vivid pastel colors...' We need punctuation that correctly closes the adverb 'respectively' after the list and separates the two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Japan, respectively,’: two commas around 'respectively' leave both independent clauses joined by only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Japan, respectively;’: the comma after 'Japan' is part of the list, 'respectively' closes the adverb, and the semicolon correctly separates the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Japan; respectively,’: the semicolon falls between 'Japan' and 'respectively,' splitting the adverb from the word it modifies and placing the boundary in the wrong location. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Japan, respectively’: no punctuation after 'respectively' fuses the two independent clauses into a run-on. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
